en_body: Your hand measures exactly the length of my body like an inchworm measuring a branch in the sky...Your arms are wide enough in Compassion to hold me lying neatly as inside a cradle...May your words know how to forgive, forgive me forever whenever I make you sad...like a gentle lullaby, all my life I am blessed because my ears may hear fully, hear wholly the lullabies my Beloved plays and sings to lull me...and it means a whole life faithful to my Beloved, my man...He forever is a gentle Love, like the Love with which he loves his beloved woman...
note: “Hạnh-nguyện” is a Buddhist term for a sacred compassionate vow and also echoes the poet's name, Hoàng-Ân.
